Module com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java
Package com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal

Class UrlValidatorImpl

java.lang.Object
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal.extension.AbstractObjectValidator<UrlValidator,URL>
com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal.UrlValidatorImpl
All Implemented Interfaces:
ExtensibleObjectValidator<UrlValidator,URL>, UrlValidator
public final class UrlValidatorImpl extends AbstractObjectValidator<UrlValidator,URL> implements UrlValidator
Default implementation of UrlValidator.

  • Constructor Details

    • UrlValidatorImpl

      public UrlValidatorImpl(ApplicationScope scope, Configuration config, String name, URL actual, List<ValidationFailure> failures, boolean fatalFailure)
      Parameters:
      scope - the application configuration
      config - the instance configuration
      name - the name of the value
      actual - the actual value
      failures - the list of validation failures
      fatalFailure - true if validation stopped as the result of a fatal failure
      Throws:
      AssertionError - if scope, config, name or failures are null. If name is blank.

  • Method Details

    • asUri

      public UriValidator asUri()
      Description copied from interface: UrlValidator
      Returns a validator for the URI representation of the value.

      Technically-speaking, there is no such thing as an invalid URI format. Per RFC3986, any String can be represented as a relative URI, but Java's implementation is based on an older specification where this was not the case.

      Specified by:
      asUri in interface UrlValidator
      Returns:
      a validator for the URI representation of the value
      See Also:
      • Discussion of Java URI vs RFC3986

    • asUri

      public UrlValidator asUri(Consumer<UriValidator> consumer)
      Description copied from interface: UrlValidator
      Validates nested requirements. This mechanism can be used to group related requirements.

      See UrlValidator.asUri() for exceptions that may be thrown to the consumer.

      Specified by:
      asUri in interface UrlValidator
      Parameters:
      consumer - validates URIs
      Returns:
      the updated validator
